questions.question: Why does image-to-image translation need an adversarial loss rather than MSE?
options: MSE is too slow to compute
There are many plausible outputs y per input x — mean-squared error flattens them into mush, while "looks real" is a sharp criterion that picks one plausible output instead of averaging them all
MSE can't handle color images
Adversarial losses need less data
questions.explanation: Sketch→photo, map→aerial, colorization — all one-to-many mappings. Pix2Pix's recipe still beats generic text-to-image models on narrow paired tasks in 2026 because paired data gives exactly the needed signal.

questions.question: What makes the discriminator in Pix2Pix "conditional"?
options: It only trains on alternate batches
D receives the PAIR (condition x, output y) — it must judge whether y is CONSISTENT with x, not merely whether y looks real in isolation
It has a conditional branch in its code
It is trained before the generator
questions.explanation: Without conditioning D, the generator could emit any realistic image regardless of the input. The pair-judging discriminator is what enforces semantic correspondence.

questions.question: Why is the generator a U-Net, and what is PatchGAN?
options: U-Net is just a fashionable name

Skip connections carry shared low-level structure (edges, silhouettes) across the bottleneck — without them high-frequency detail vanishes; PatchGAN outputs an N×N grid where each cell judges a ~70×70 receptive field, assuming realism is LOCAL
U-Net halves the parameter count
PatchGAN classifies entire datasets at once
questions.explanation: The patch-based Markov-random-field assumption makes D faster to train with fewer parameters and produces sharper output than one global real/fake score.

questions.question: Why add an L1 term (λ=100) to the Pix2Pix generator loss, and why L1 over L2?
options: L1 is the only differentiable choice
L1 stabilizes training and pulls G toward the known paired target; L1 produces sharper edges than L2 because it corresponds to medians rather than means
It replaces the adversarial loss entirely
L2 cannot be computed on images
questions.explanation: Interesting detail: Pix2Pix has no input noise z — Isola found explicit noise got ignored, so stochasticity comes from dropout inside G.

questions.question: How does CycleGAN remove the paired-data requirement?
options: By generating its own pairs with GPT
Two generators G: X→Y and F: Y→X trained with cycle-consistency losses F(G(x)) ≈ x and G(F(y)) ≈ y — enabling horse→zebra or summer→winter translation with no paired examples
By training twice as long on one domain
It doesn't — it needs pairs too
questions.explanation: In 2026 unpaired image-to-image mostly runs through diffusion (ControlNet, IP-Adapter), but cycle consistency survives in nearly every unpaired domain-adaptation paper.
